topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the spreadsheet, the numbers swimming before his eyes. He had spent the last hour meticulously cross-referencing project allocations, a task he was certain he had executed flawlessly. Then he saw it: Sarah’s salary bracket, juxtaposed against his own. A cold, tight knot began to form in his stomach, tightening with every glance at the figures. When Sarah approached his desk to ask a quick question about the quarterly reports, Liam managed a strained smile. “Sure, Sarah. Let me just pull that up for you.” His voice sounded unnaturally high. He felt his jaw clench so hard his molars ached. He focused intensely on the monitor, willing the discrepancy to simply vanish, unable to meet her gaze.

[story 2]

“That’s… quite a difference,” Mark murmured, the words barely escaping his lips as he reviewed the payroll summary. He felt a sudden, sharp prickle behind his eyes. He looked up, meeting Chloe’s gaze across the crowded breakroom. Chloe, who was currently laughing easily with a senior manager, offered a bright, breezy smile. Mark felt a sudden urge to shrink into his chair, pulling his shoulders inward as if trying to make himself smaller. When Chloe finally turned her attention to him, Mark offered a weak nod, his hands gripping his lukewarm coffee mug so tightly the ceramic threatened to crack. He focused on the condensation running down the side of the mug, anything but the reality of the disparity laid bare on the screen.

[story 3]

"Did you see the compensation structure for the new hires?" David asked, his tone deliberately casual, though the edge of forced nonchalance was palpable. He felt a distinct heat rising in his neck. He looked at Maria, who was organizing files nearby, her movements efficient and unhurried. David deliberately kept his expression neutral, but his fingers tapped a restless rhythm on the edge of his desk. When Maria finally looked up, David offered a curt, clipped reply. “I’ll review the documentation later,” he said, his voice flat, the effort to sound unaffected costing him visible strain. He immediately turned back to his screen, focusing on a single pixel, his breathing becoming shallow and quick.
